auth.signInSubtitle: Open your workspace and continue the runs already in motion.
auth.forgotPasswordSent: If an account exists for that email, we've sent a reset link. Check your inbox.
auth.invalidResetLink: This reset link is invalid or has expired.
auth.orgNotFoundHint: We couldn't find an organization with that identifier.
auth.passwordTooWeak: Password doesn't meet the strength requirements.
auth.surfaceTitle: Run agents with memory, tools, and approvals in one place.
auth.surfaceSubtitle: CubePlex keeps long-running agent work inside the same workspace as your models, connectors, artifacts, and team controls.
auth.surfacePanelDescription: Sign in to inspect active runs, review tool access, and resume work with full workspace context.
auth.surfaceFeatureMemoryDescription: Keep useful context available without rebuilding every prompt.
auth.surfaceFeatureToolsDescription: Connect MCP services, sandbox work, and internal systems.
auth.surfaceFeatureControlDescription: Use approvals, scopes, and audit trails before work leaves the box.
auth.surfaceSignalRuns: Streaming runs stay attached to their workspace.
auth.surfaceSignalScopes: Org and workspace scopes stay separate.
auth.surfaceSignalAudit: Artifacts, citations, and decisions stay reviewable.

invite.title: Invite to this chat
invite.description: Add workspace members so they can see and reply in this conversation.

upgradeDialog.externalBindingError: This conversation is bound to an IM, schedule, or trigger and cannot be upgraded.

home.subtitle: Agents do the work. Teams stay in control.

document.title: Create a document or deck
document.description: Turn source material into a Word document or presentation.
document.prompt:
Create a polished [Word document / presentation] from the topic and source material below. First organize the structure for the intended audience, then produce an editable file.

Output format:
Audience:
Topic and source material:

analyze.title: Analyze a spreadsheet
analyze.description: Clean Excel or CSV data, find patterns, and create charts.
analyze.prompt:
Analyze the attached spreadsheet. Clean the data, identify key trends and anomalies, and create clear charts with a concise summary the team can act on.

[Attach an Excel or CSV file]

automate.title: Automate recurring work
automate.description: Connect systems and run repeatable work with approvals.
automate.prompt:
Help me automate the recurring workflow below. First confirm which systems and data you need to access, what actions are allowed, and which steps require my approval. Then propose the workflow and proceed within those boundaries.

Workflow:

adminAuthentication.title: Authentication
adminAuthentication.subtitle: Configure SSO for your organization. When SSO is active, password login is disabled for all members.

empty.title: Single sign-on isn't set up yet
empty.body: Connect an OIDC or SAML identity provider so members can sign in with your IdP instead of a password.

status.label: Status
status.testingHelp: Save the configuration, then test the SSO flow before activating. While testing, password login still works.
status.activeHelp: SSO is enforced. Members must sign in via your identity provider.
status.inactiveHelp: SSO is configured but turned off. Password login is in effect.

form.displayNameHelp: Shown on the org login page next to the SSO button.
form.provisioningAutoHelp: Create a member account on the first successful SSO login.
form.provisioningInviteHelp: Only members invited beforehand can sign in.

oidc.discoverHelp: Fetches /.well-known/openid-configuration and fills the endpoints below.
oidc.clientSecretPlaceholder: Leave blank to keep the existing secret
oidc.scopesHelp: Space-separated. The defaults work for most providers.
oidc.redirectUriHelp: Add this exact URL to your IdP's allowed redirect URIs.

saml.metadataUploadTodo: Metadata upload coming soon — paste the values manually for now.

attributeMapping.help: How fields from your IdP map to CubePlex accounts.

validation.endpointsRequired: Use Discover or fill the authorization, token, and JWKS fields.

errors.sso_already_configured: An SSO connection already exists for this organization.
errors.invalid_issuer_url: The issuer URL didn't return a valid OIDC discovery document.
errors.oidc_discovery_failed: OIDC discovery failed. Check the issuer URL.
errors.oidc_discovery_refused: Refused: the URL must use https and resolve to a public address.
errors.invalid_config: The SSO configuration is invalid. Review the fields above.
errors.config_url_refused: A configured URL points to a non-public address. Use a public https endpoint.
errors.client_secret_required_for_oidc: OIDC connections need a client secret before activation.
errors.deactivate_before_delete: Deactivate the SSO connection before deleting it.
errors.invalid_status_transition: That status change isn't allowed from the current state.
errors.app_base_url_missing_scheme: The deployment's app base URL is missing a scheme. Contact your operator.
errors.unauthorized: You don't have permission to change SSO settings.
errors.forbidden: You don't have permission to change SSO settings.

panel.testSsoHelp: Opens the org login page in a new tab so you can run the SSO flow end-to-end. Raw IdP attributes next to the mapped values are coming soon.
panel.activateConfirmBody: Password login will be disabled for all members. They must sign in through your identity provider. Make sure the test flow worked first.
panel.deactivateConfirmBody: Members will no longer be able to sign in via SSO. Password login will be re-enabled.
panel.deleteConfirmBody: This permanently removes the IdP configuration. Linked identities will be unlinked, but member accounts remain.
panel.validateFailed: Some checks failed — see details below

identities.unlinkConfirmBody: {email} will need to re-link their account the next time they sign in via SSO.

memory.title: Memory
memory.description: Memories your AI assistant has built up over time in this workspace.

sandboxEnv.fieldValueEdit: New value (leave blank to keep current)
sandboxEnv.typeSecretDesc: Injected as a placeholder. The egress proxy substitutes the real value into outbound HTTP request headers at runtime. Requires allowed hosts.
sandboxEnv.typeEnvDesc: The actual value is injected directly into the sandbox env var. Encrypted at rest.
sandboxEnv.validNameFormat: Use letters, digits, or underscores; must start with a letter or underscore
sandboxEnv.validHostRequired: At least one host is required for secrets

adminPresets.label: Label
adminPresets.chainEmpty: Add at least one model ref (slug/model_id).
adminPresets.taskPresetsHint: Optional: route specific internal tasks through a named preset. Leave unset to use the default preset.
adminPresets.errorBrokenPreset: One or more chain entries reference models that don't exist. Fix the highlighted refs and try again.
adminPresets.errorTierMissingPrimary: Every enabled tier needs a primary model.
adminPresets.errorCustomMissingPrimary: Custom presets need a primary model.
adminPresets.errorLabelCollidesTier: Label can't match a tier name.
adminPresets.errorDefaultUnavailable: Pick a default from an enabled tier or a complete custom preset.

lite.name: Lite
lite.description: Fastest and cheapest. Best for simple, high-volume tasks.

flash.name: Flash
flash.description: Fast with solid quality. A balanced everyday choice.

pro.name: Pro
pro.description: Recommended. Strong reasoning for most work.

max.name: Max
max.description: Most capable. For the hardest, highest-stakes tasks.

taskRouting.heading: Task routing
taskRouting.hint: Route specific internal tasks through a preset. Leave on default to use the default preset.

compaction.name: Compaction
compaction.hint: Compresses context when the chat gets long.

adminSandbox.subtitle: Set the default image and resource limits.

adminExt.unknownBodyPrefix: Could not find an extension named

shellLayout.deleteConversationDescription: "{title}" will be removed from your history. Messages will no longer be available in this conversation. Related artifacts will no longer appear in the library or the conversation panel.

triggers.title: Triggers
triggers.subtitle: Run prompts automatically when events arrive via webhook.
triggers.deleteConfirm: Are you sure? This will permanently delete the trigger and all its event history.
triggers.emptyHint: Create a trigger to get an ingest URL, then paste it into your event source's webhook settings.
triggers.selectHint: Select a trigger to view its details.
triggers.fieldWebhookSecretHint: Save this secret now — we won't show it again.
triggers.fieldPromptTemplateHint: Use [[ jsonpath ]] style placeholders for whitelisted payload fields (double curly braces in the actual template).
triggers.fieldPayloadFieldsHint: Comma-separated JSONPath expressions. Only these fields are interpolated into the template.
triggers.destNewEachTimeHint: Every ingested event creates its own conversation. Pick a topic to keep them grouped.
triggers.destImChannelHint: Posts events into a chat in your linked IM platform.
triggers.destImChannelDisabledHint: Created from IM only — bind a channel through your chat platform.
triggers.destTopicHint: Optional. New conversations created from this trigger will be placed under this topic.
triggers.destinationLabelDetailHint: Where each ingested event's conversation lives. Locked after creation.
triggers.ingestUrlHint: Paste this URL into your source provider's webhook settings.
triggers.secretManagementHint: Rotate the webhook secret with an overlap window to avoid dropped events.
triggers.enabledSwitchHint: Disable to stop processing incoming events without deleting the trigger.
triggers.saveSecretWarning: Save the new secret now — we won't show it again after rotation.
triggers.overlapSecondsHint: The old secret remains valid for this many seconds after rotation. Default: 86400 (24h).

mcpAdmin.customTestOkOAuth: OAuth metadata reachable · authorize to list tools

runError.context_length_exceeded: Conversation exceeded {model}'s context window ({tokens_in, number} / {context_window, number} tokens). Start a new chat or switch models.
runError.rate_limited: Rate limit hit for {model}. Wait a moment, then try again.
runError.provider_auth_failed: Authentication with {provider} failed. Check your API key in workspace settings.
runError.provider_unavailable: {provider} is unavailable right now. Try again shortly.
runError.provider_bad_request: {provider} rejected the request. Open this run's details to see the raw error.
runError.tool_failed: A tool call failed during this turn. Open this run's details to see the raw error.
runError.internal_error: Something went wrong. Open this run's details to see the raw error.

scheduledTasks.pageSubtitle: Run prompts automatically on a daily, weekly, interval, or one-time schedule
scheduledTasks.emptyHint: Create a task to run prompts on a schedule automatically.
scheduledTasks.selectHint: Select a task to view its run history.
scheduledTasks.name: Name
scheduledTasks.promptPlaceholder: Summarize today's news and send me a digest…
scheduledTasks.targetNewEachRunHint: Each run creates a fresh conversation. Pick a topic to keep them grouped.
scheduledTasks.targetFixedHint: Runs always append to one existing conversation you own.
scheduledTasks.targetImChannelHint: Posts to a chat in your linked IM platform.
scheduledTasks.targetImChannelDisabledHint: Create from IM, or edit an existing task bound to an IM conversation/topic.
scheduledTasks.targetImChannelRetargetHint: Uses the IM binding of this task's conversation or topic. Fails if none is found.
scheduledTasks.targetImChannelRetargetNote: No channel picker — the server resolves the binding from the task's conversation or topic. If there is no IMThreadLink, save will fail.
scheduledTasks.targetImChannelCreateOnlyFromIm: IM channel destination can only be chosen when editing a task that has an IM-bound conversation or topic.
scheduledTasks.targetLockedAfterCreate: Destination can't be changed after the task is created.
scheduledTasks.targetTopicHint: Optional. New conversations are placed under this topic, inheriting its members and sandbox.
scheduledTasks.conversationIdHint: Must be one of your own conversations
scheduledTasks.imDestinationReadOnlyHint: This task was created from an IM message — only the prompt and schedule can be edited.
scheduledTasks.dayOfMonthHint: Days 1–28 avoid short-month skips; the last-day option adapts to each month's length
scheduledTasks.legacyCron: This task uses a custom cron expression:
scheduledTasks.switchToVisual: Switch to the visual editor (clears the cron expression)

prereq.intents: Enable the Privileged Gateway Intent below in Bot settings
prereq.invite: Copy the generated URL and open it to add the bot to your server
prereq.scopes: In OAuth2 → URL Generator, select these scopes
prereq.permissions: In the Bot Permissions section below, grant these permissions

prereq.app: Create a Slack App at api.slack.com/apps
prereq.scopes: Add these Bot Token Scopes in OAuth & Permissions
prereq.socketMode: Enable Socket Mode in the app settings
prereq.appToken: Generate an App-Level Token with this scope
prereq.install: Install the app to your workspace and copy the Bot Token

prereq.app: Create a Bot Registration in Azure Portal (or via Teams Toolkit)
prereq.graphPermission: Add User.Read.All application permission and grant admin consent
prereq.clientSecret: Create a Client Secret in Certificates & Secrets

prereq.app: Create an internal app on open.dingtalk.com (Developer Backend → Apps)
prereq.stream: Enable Stream Mode (no public callback URL required)
prereq.permissions: Grant these permissions in the app's Permissions panel
prereq.credentials: Copy AppKey and AppSecret from the app's Credentials page

verifyBody.pressConnect: Press Connect to hydrate the bot identity and open the connection.

field.appIdFormat: App ID must start with cli_
field.appSecretBad: Bad credentials. Re-copy from the developer console.

banner.hydrationFailed: Could not verify the bot identity. Make sure the app version is published and the bot is enabled in the Feishu console.

routing.platformOnlyPerPerson: This platform only supports per-person routing.

routing.body: Only affects group chats — DMs are always one-on-one. In a group, should the bot keep each person's chat separate, or treat the whole group as one shared conversation?
routing.isolated: Each member talks to the bot privately. In the CubePlex web UI, you only see your own thread; nobody else in the channel can see what you asked.
routing.shared: The whole channel shares one CubePlex conversation. Everyone in the group can see what others ask the bot and build on each other's questions.

sandbox.body: Only matters for shared (group) conversations — choose whose sandbox the bot uses to run code, edit files, and keep state. In DMs the bot always uses the DM user's own sandbox.
sandbox.dedicated: This group conversation gets a brand-new sandbox of its own, isolated from any other work.
sandbox.creator: Reuses the personal sandbox of the CubePlex user who set up this IM bot (not the IM channel's creator). Useful when you want this group chat to share files and state with that user's other CubePlex work.

topic.body: A topic is a CubePlex container that groups multiple conversations together — think of it as a folder in the chat sidebar. This setting decides what happens when you /new with the bot in IM.
topic.topic: Every /new opens a new conversation, but they all stay folded under one topic in the sidebar.
topic.flat: Each /new becomes its own standalone item in the chat history, listed flat.

routing.isolated: Each IM user talks to the bot privately — in CubePlex, each member sees only their own exchanges with the bot, not other members'.
routing.shared: Everyone in the channel shares one conversation — anyone in the group can see what others ask the bot and build on it.

topic.isolatedTopic: In CubePlex, each IM user's conversations are folded under their own topic folder — visible only to that user.
topic.sharedTopic: In CubePlex, the channel's conversations are folded under one shared topic folder — visible to everyone in the group.
topic.flat: In CubePlex's chat history, conversations appear flat — no topic folder.

sandbox.isolated: The bot runs code and edits files in each IM user's own CubePlex sandbox — sandboxes are isolated between users.
sandbox.dedicated: The bot runs code and edits files in a dedicated sandbox owned by this group conversation, isolated from any other work.
sandbox.creator: The bot runs code and edits files in the bot owner's personal sandbox, shared with that user's other CubePlex work.

dm.label: In DMs (one-on-one)
dm.base: The bot talks to each IM user privately and runs code in that user's own personal sandbox.

topic.topic: In CubePlex, each user's DM conversations are folded under their own topic folder named “{botName}” — visible only to that user.
topic.flat: In CubePlex's chat history, DM conversations appear flat — no topic folder.

identityLinks.title: Linked users
identityLinks.empty: No users linked yet. IM users can run /link to bind their account.

deleteDialog.title: Delete this bot connection?
deleteDialog.body: This removes the credential and stops any active connection. The {platform} app itself is untouched.

link.title: Link IM Account
link.success: Your {platform} account has been linked to CubePlex successfully.
link.invalidToken: This link is invalid or has expired. Please run /link again in your IM.
link.emailMismatch: Please log in with the email specified in the /link command.
link.notMember: You are not a member of this workspace. Ask the workspace admin to add you.

wsSandboxes.title: Sandboxes
wsSandboxes.subtitle: Your isolated sandbox environments in this workspace.
wsSandboxes.emptyHint: Sandboxes appear here once you start a conversation in this workspace.
wsSandboxes.intro: Each sandbox is an isolated environment tied to a conversation or topic. Restarting keeps your files; deleting clears everything (operator reclaims storage).
wsSandboxes.restartConfirmBody: The container will be stopped and a fresh one starts on next use. Your files are kept.
wsSandboxes.deleteConfirmBody: Permanently deletes this sandbox and its container. Stored files are left for your operator to reclaim. This cannot be undone.
